text:
In a small coastal town, there lived a quirky old owl named Olive. Olive wasn't just any old owl; she had a flair for the dramatic, and every autumn she'd wear a specially embroidered cloak depicting scenes from various maritime adventures.

This particular fall, Olive decided to host a Halloween party at her hollow. She decorated with ghostly lanterns and had a costume contest—owl-sized macnets complete with fake feathers. On the guest list were the usual assortment of nocturnal creatures but also some peculiar characters who had somehow ended up wanting to join the owl community.

There was Mr. Fangywig, always trying out ridiculous costumes that scared everyone except himself. Then there was Greedy Greby—the squirrel who ate everything but enjoyed no dessert. Finally, there was Harold Halstead, the only bat who didn't fly. Every year Harold just swung around in his blanket fort, munching on ancient garlic snaps.

The night of the party, as the moon began its vigil over the pond,
